refined_fr.refined_fr: The NGHE solution shall ensure teleoperation communication latency p95 ≤ 150 ms end-to-end to enable safe remote operation.
refined_fr.rationale: The original FR specifies teleoperation latency compatibility with safe remote operation. Refining it to a measurable latency threshold (p95 ≤ 150 ms) aligns with acceptance criteria and safety criticality.
refined_fr.verification_idea: Measure end-to-end latency under agreed network conditions using telemetry data during teleoperation sessions and verify p95 latency ≤ 150 ms.
sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the network link between operator and vehicle.
FR2: Ensure real-time processing and forwarding of teleoperation commands and telemetry within the NGHE solution.
FR3: Provide latency monitoring and alerting to detect and mitigate latency breaches.

candidate_dps: DP1: Use optimized network protocols and QoS prioritization for teleoperation traffic.
DP2: Implement edge computing nodes close to vehicle to reduce processing delays.
DP3: Design telemetry and tasking data formats for minimal serialization overhead.
DP4: Integrate latency monitoring and adaptive control to maintain latency within threshold.
selected_dp.selected_dp: DP1: Use optimized network protocols and QoS prioritization for teleoperation traffic.

potential_sources: Tight coupling between network protocol optimization and telemetry data formats.
coupling_check.notes: DP1 focuses on network protocol and QoS which logically maps to communication latency control and can be modularized separately from telemetry data format design.

assumptions_risks: Assumes network infrastructure supports QoS and protocol optimizations.
Risk of latency spikes due to unpredictable network conditions outside system control.
Assumes telemetry and tasking data formats are compatible with optimized protocols.
selected_dp.recommended_revision: Consider integrating DP3 and DP4 in subsequent decomposition levels to address serialization overhead and latency monitoring explicitly.

im_boundary.name: Teleoperation
im_boundary.scope: Module responsible for managing teleoperation communication, including protocol optimization, QoS handling, and real-time command/telemetry exchange.

external_interfaces.name: Network Link
external_interfaces.details: Physical and logical network connection carrying teleoperation data between operator and vehicle.

external_interfaces.name: Telemetry
external_interfaces.details: Interface for sending and receiving telemetry data relevant to teleoperation latency monitoring.

external_interfaces.name: Tasking
external_interfaces.details: Interface for sending teleoperation commands and receiving acknowledgments.

module_containment_scores.notes: Broad scope but less focused containment of teleoperation latency logic.
Vehicle module contains hardware but limited direct control over communication protocols.
Highest containment score; directly responsible for teleoperation communication and latency management.
Safety services monitor alerts but do not control communication latency.

assigned_im.containment_hypothesis: Assigning DP1 to Teleoperation module centralizes teleoperation communication latency control, minimizing diffusion and improving ownership clarity.
containment_risks: Potential overlap with NGHE Solution module if network infrastructure management is shared.
Dependency on Network Link interface performance outside Teleoperation module control.
assigned_im.recommended_revision: Clarify boundaries between Teleoperation and NGHE Solution modules regarding network infrastructure responsibilities.

fr_dp_gate.reason: Selected DP (optimized network protocols and QoS) directly addresses the refined FR of minimizing teleoperation latency with measurable criteria.

diffusion_risks: Some diffusion risk exists if network infrastructure responsibilities are split between Teleoperation and NGHE Solution modules.
dp_im_gate.containment_notes: Teleoperation module shows highest containment score and clear ownership of latency control logic.

fr_im_gate.verification_feasibility: Latency can be verified end-to-end via telemetry and network monitoring tools interfacing with Teleoperation module.
fr_im_gate.allocation_clarity: Clear allocation of latency control DP to Teleoperation IM with well-defined interfaces.

recommended_revisions: Define explicit interface contracts between Teleoperation and NGHE Solution modules for network infrastructure management.
Plan subsequent decomposition to address telemetry data format optimization and latency monitoring.

decision.justification: While the current decomposition is sound, further refinement is needed to clarify network infrastructure responsibilities and to incorporate telemetry data format and latency monitoring DPs in the next level.
next_level_sub_frs: FR1: Minimize communication latency in the network link between operator and vehicle.
FR2: Ensure real-time processing and forwarding of teleoperation commands and telemetry within the NGHE solution.
FR3: Provide latency monitoring and alerting to detect and mitigate latency breaches.

revise.why: To explicitly include telemetry data format optimization and latency monitoring DPs and clarify coupling with network infrastructure.

assumptions: Network infrastructure supports QoS and protocol optimizations.
Telemetry and tasking data formats are compatible with optimized protocols.
open_risks: Latency spikes due to unpredictable network conditions outside system control.
Potential diffusion of responsibilities between Teleoperation and NGHE Solution modules.
evidence_needed: Performance data from network infrastructure under QoS configurations.
Verification results of telemetry and tasking data serialization overhead.
